  • VIPまたはプレミアム参加者層
  • プロフェッショナルイベントプランナーのための購読
  • 分析と洞察プレミアム

Rork利点： イベントアプリは多くの移動部分を調整します。チケット販売、ロジスティクス、コミュニケーション、追跡。Rorkは技術的複雑さを処理し、ユーザー体験とコミュニティ成長に焦点を当てることができます。

10. 不動産 / プロパティリスティングアプリ

**それが何：**プロパティ買収者、売主、賃借人を包括的なリスト情報とツールで接続するマーケットプレイス。

Rorkが生成できる主要機能：

  • 写真、ビデオ、3Dツアー付きプロパティリスト
  • 詳細なプロパティ情報（価格、場所、仕様）
  • プロパティの位置を表示するマップ統合
  • 高度な検索とフィルタリング（価格、寝室、メンヤムス）
  • 保存されたお気に入りと比較ツール
  • 住宅ローン計算機と資金調達ツール
  • 地区情報とウォーク性スコア
  • エージェントの連絡とメッセージング
  • 仮想ツアーまたはビデオのウォークスルー
  • プロパティ履歴と市場トレンド
  • 新しいリストのための通知アラート
  • エージェントまたは投資家の分析ダッシュボード

なぜそれが重要か： 不動産はグローバルに最大の市場の1つです。プロパティ発見を簡単にし、より透明にするアプリは、買い手、売主、エージェントに多大な価値を作成します。平均的な家の購入はほとんどの人が作る最大の財政決定です。彼らはより良いツールに支払う意思がある。

ターゲットオーディエンス：

  • 個人的なブランドを構築する不動産エージェント
  • 顧客向けプラットフォームを作成するブローカレッジ
  • ポートフォリオを追跡する不動産投資家
  • 新しいプロパティをショーケースする開発者
  • ニッチな市場（商用、贅沢、農村不動産）

マネタイズオプション：

  • プレミアムエージェントリスト
  • トランザクション手数料
  • エージェントツールのためのサブスクリプション
  • 仮想ツアー作成サービス
  • 購入者/売主の問い合わせからのリード生成
  • 市場参加者に販売されたデータ分析

Rork利点： 不動産アプリは、複雑な視覚化要件を持つ膨大なデータを管理します。Rorkはマップ統合、検索アルゴリズム、メディア処理を生成します。エージェントパートナーシップとユーザー取得に焦点を当てることができます。

あなたのユースケースの開始

これら10個のユースケースはそれぞれ実行可能なビジネス機会を表しています。彼らはおよそ最も単純なものから最も複雑なものまで順序付けられていますが、すべてはRorkで達成可能です。

以下に基づいて選択：

  1. **あなたの専門知識：**どのドメインをあなたは親密に知っていますか？
  2. **市場ニーズ：**真の痛みポイントはどこを参照していますか？
  3. **マネタイズの実行可能性：**ユーザー/ビジネスは継続的にあなたの収益モデルを維持できますか？
  4. **競争：**十分な差別化機会がありますか？
  5. **あなたの情熱：**6ヶ月以上の間、これを構築する動機を保つでしょうか？

Rorkの美しい側面は、1つのユースケースを選ぶことは永続的ではないということです。最初のアプリを構築し、何が機能し、何がそうでないかを学び、その後、その知識を2番目のアプリで使用します。最初のアプリでのあなたの経験により、2番目のアプリは大幅に高速で利益が出ます。

これらのすべてのユースケースにおけるRork利点

どちらを選択しても、Rorkが提供します：

  • **速度：**数ヶ月ではなく数週間でMVPを起動
  • **ポーランド：**プロフェッショナルな美学とスムーズなインタラクション組み込み
  • **スケーラビリティ：**小さく開始し、完全な書き直しなしで成長
  • **費用対効果：**採用なしで利益が出るビジネスを構築
  • **柔軟性：**ユーザーフィードバックに基づいて迅速に反復

大規模な予算、大規模なチーム、または広範なコーディング経験を必要とするアプリを構築する時代は終わりです。これら10個のユースケースは、その可能な方法の始まりのみを表しています。

1つを選んでください。今日の構築を開始してください。あなたの利益が出るアプリはあなたを待っています。

ja.title: 個人開発者のためのRork活用術：アプリビジネスの始め方
ja.author: Rork Lab
ja.description: 個人開発者がRorkを使って利益が出るアプリを構築する方法。マネタイズ戦略と成長戦略を解説。
ja.content:
個人開発者のためのRork活用術：アプリビジネスの始め方

完全なクリエイティブコントロールを保ちながら利益が出るアプリを構築し、すべての収益を自分のものにするという夢は、もはや深いポケットを持つ経験豊富なチームの専有物ではありません。Rorkで武装した個人開発者は、プロフェッショナルなアプリケーションを起動し、迅速にアイデアを検証し、完全に独立して持続可能な収入を構築することができるようになりました。

ソロ開発者にRorkが理想的な理由

従来のアプリ開発は、ソロ開発者を厳しい状況に追いやります：開発者を雇う（高額で時間がかかる）、広範なコーディングを学ぶ（数ヶ月の学習）、または品質と機能を妥協する。Rorkはこのジレンマを完全に排除します。

速度はあなたのスーパーパワー

従来のアプリ構築には、コーディング、デバッグ、反復に数ヶ月かかります。Rorkは大幅にこのタイムラインを圧縮します。アイデアから動作するプロトタイプまで、数ヶ月ではなく数日で実現できます。複数のアプリを構築する際にはこのスピード利点は複合します。2番目のアプリは最初のアプリよりも短い時間で完成します。なぜなら、あなたはプロセスを洗練させたからです。

チームは必要ありません

共同創設者の雇用や管理とは異なり、必要なのはあなた自身だけです。給与を支払わず、人格を管理せず、株式分割をナビゲートする必要はありません。すべての決定はあなたのもの。すべての収益はあなたに流れます。Rorkはアプリ開発を民主化し、ソロ操業を実行可能で利益が出るようにします。

サイドプロジェクトの柔軟性

ほとんどのソロ開発者は他の場所で雇用されながら開始します。Rorkはこの現実に対応しています。自分のペースで構築できます。インスピレーションを得たときの激しいスプリント、忙しい季節の遅い進捗。プロの結果にはプロのスケジュールは不要です。

完全な開発前にアプリのアイデアを検証する

すべてのアプリのアイデアが成功するわけではありません。フル機能のリリースに大量の時間を投資する前に、人々が実際にあなたのソリューションを望んでいることを検証してください。

検証フレームワーク

1. 調査フェーズ（1週目）

  • ターゲットオーディエンスを正確に特定
  • あなたのカテゴリの既存アプリを検索
  • 競争するアプリのレビューを読む。ユーザーは何に不満を持っていますか？
  • あなたのターゲット市場内の20～30人を見つけて、彼らのニーズについて話し合いたい

2. Rorkを使用してプロトタイプを作成（週2～3）

  • あなたの中核的価値提案を実証する簡略されたプロトタイプを構築
  • あなたのアプリを異なるにする1つの機能に焦点を当てる
  • すべてを構築しないでください。Rorkはあなたが必須にフォーカスするのに役立ちます
  • 過度な設計を避けるのに十分なポーランド

3. テスト（4週目）

  • ターゲットオーディエンスとプロトタイプを共有
  • 特定のメトリクスを追跡：実際に使用する人はどれくらい？どのくらいの期間？彼らは戻ってくるのか？
  • 具体的なフィードバックを収集：「これを定期的に使用するために何が必要ですか？」
  • 取引ブレーカーを特定：どの機能が譲れないのか？

4. 決定ポイント

  • 3日後のエンゲージメントが低下した場合、アイデアはおそらく持続力がない
  • ユーザーが継続的に使用し、特定の機能をリクエストする場合、需要を検証しました
  • 反応がぬるい場合でも、明確なオーディエンスセグメントがそれを愛する場合、ニッチを見つけました

この検証プロセスは2～3週間かかり、最小限のお金を費やします。検証なしでフルアプリを立ち上げることは、誰も望まないものに浪費された数ヶ月の努力をリスクにさらします。

実際に機能するマネタイズ戦略

1. フリーミアムモデル：最大のユーザー成長、遅延収益

仕組み：

  • アプリ全体は無料で使用できます
  • 高度な機能にはサブスクリプションまたは1回限りの購入が必要です
  • ユーザーはすぐに価値を経験します。自然にアップグレードします

最適な用途：

  • 生産性アプリ（タスクマネージャー、ノートテーカー）
  • コンテンツ作成ツール
  • フィットネスと健康アプリ
  • ソーシャルまたはコミュニティ機能

Rorkでの実装：

  • 寛大な無料層を構築（99％の機能は支払いなしでアクセス可能）
  • 1つの強力な機能はアップグレードトリガーを作成します
  • ユーザーが無料制限に達した場合、自然に表示されるアプリ内プロンプト

収益可能性：

  • フリーミアムは通常、ユーザーの2～5％を有料顧客に変換
  • 10,000無料ダウンロード→200～500有料加入者
  • 月額$5：月間$1,000～$2,500の収益

利点：

  • 成長は無制限です；ペイウォールはユーザー取得を妨げません
  • より大きいユーザーベースは認識価値を増加させ、社会的証拠を提供します

2. サブスクリプションモデル：予測可能な定期的収益

仕組み：

  • コア機能には月次または年次サブスクリプションが必要です
  • ユーザーはアプリ自体にアクセスするために支払う
  • 複数のレベル選択肢は異なるユースケースに対応

最適な用途：

  • プロフェッショナルツール（プロジェクト管理、CRM）
  • 専門的なアプリケーション（株式追跡、言語学習）
  • 継続的な価値の提供を伴うサービス
  • 継続的な更新により改善するアプリ

Rorkでの実装：

  • クレジットカード不要で3～7日間の無料トライアルを提供
  • ティア価格：Basic（$5/月）、Professional（$15/月）、Enterprise（$50/月）
  • マーケティングで時間の節約または特定の利点を強調
  • 年間プランを20％割引で長いコミットメントを促進

収益可能性：

  • サブスクリプション顧客は顧客あたり12倍の年間リターンを生成
  • 月額$10で100人の加入者=年間$12,000の収益
  • 毎月80％のカスタマーを保持すると予測可能な成長を作成

利点：

  • 非常に予測可能な収益がビジネス計画を可能にします
  • 顧客ライフタイムバリュー報酬顧客保持努力

3. 1回限りの購入：シンプルさと高いマージン

仕組み：

  • ユーザーはアプリを永遠に所有するために1回支払う
  • 継続的なサブスクリプションは必要ありません
  • ユーティリティアプリとツールに最適

最適な用途：

  • スタンドアロンユーティリティ（単位コンバーター、計算機）
  • コンテンツアプリ（ebook リーダー、学習モジュール）
  • マルチプレイヤー機能のないゲーム
  • 参考ツール

Rorkでの実装：

  • 価格ポイント：認識価値に応じて$0.99～$9.99
  • 限定的な機能を持つ1つの無料ライトバージョンを提供
  • アプリが有用なままであるために継続的な更新を必要としないことを確認
  • クチコミと有機的な発見に焦点を当てる

収益可能性：

  • 1,000ダウンロード：$4.99=$4,990収益
  • 1回限りの購入はカスタマー取得の複雑さを排除します
  • ユーザーあたりの収益は少ないですが、カスタマーサポートコストは著しく低い

利点：

  • ユーザーは本当に何かを所有していると感じます
  • サブスクリプション疲労とチャーン懸念を排除します

4. 広告サポートモデル：エンゲージメントからの収益

仕組み：

  • アプリは完全に無料です
  • 控えめな広告はインプレッションとクリックから収益を生成
  • マネタイズとユーザーエクスペリエンスのバランス調整

最適な用途：

  • カジュアルオーディエンスを持つゲーム
  • コンテンツの多いアプリ（ニュース、メディア）
  • 高い日々のアクティブユーザーを持つツール
  • サブスクリプションが一般的でない新興市場をターゲットとするアプリ

Rorkでの実装：

  • 確立されたネットワークを使用（Google AdMob、Facebook Audience Network）
  • 広告を慎重に配置し、ペニーのためにユーザーエクスペリエンスを犠牲にしないでください
  • インタースティシャル広告はセッション間でバナーより効果的です
  • リワード広告（オプション、ユーザーが開始）はより高いエンゲージメント生成

収益可能性：

  • ニッチとトラフィック品質によって大きく異なります
  • 保守的な推定：千インプレッションあたり$0.50～$2.00
  • 月間100,000インプレッション=$50～$200収益
  • ウイルスゲームは月間$1,000～$5,000を生成できます

利点：

  • ユーザー取得のための摩擦なし
  • 収益ユーザー成長に応じてスケール
  • 他のマネタイズと組み合わせ可能（広告+プレミアム機能）

支払いシステムの統合

どのマネタイズモデルを選択しても、信頼できる支払い処理が必要です。

Stripe統合

サブスクリプションと1回限りの購入に最適です。

セットアップ：

  • Stripeアカウントを作成（15分）
  • Rork生成バックエンドに接続
  • 商品価格と請求サイクルを構成

利点：

  • 優れたドキュメントを備えた業界標準
  • 異なる地域の税金計算を処理
  • クレジットカード、デジタルウォレット、国際支払いをサポート
  • 透明な価格：トランザクションあたり2.9％+ $0.30

Rork統合：

  • Rorkは支払いフロー接続にRorkチェックアウトを生成できます
  • 自動請求サイクル管理
  • サブスクリプション状態の変更のためのWebhook統合

アプリ内購入（AppleとGoogle）

iOSおよびAndroidのマネタイズに不可欠です。

iOS App Store：

  • StoreKit 2（Appleのフレームワーク）を使用
  • Rorkは購入ロジックと領収書検証を生成できます
  • Appleが30％の手数料を取ります。あなたは70％を取得します

Google Play Store：

  • Billing Library v6+を使用
  • Appleと同様の30/70スプリット（Googleは30％を取得）
  • Appleより複雑な領収書検証

実装上の考慮事項：

  • 両方のプラットフォームは特定の技術実装が必要
  • Rorkは必要なコード構造を生成できます
  • 起動前に徹底的にテスト。購入ロジックはセキュリティに敏感です

ハイブリッドアプローチ

サブスクリプション（より良いユーザー管理と透明性）にStripeを使用し、1回限りのコンテンツ購入またはプレミアム機能にアプリ内購入を使用します。この柔軟性はしばしば単一のマネタイズ方法より優れたユーザー変換します。

アプリをマーケティングする：App Store Optimization（ASO）

検出がなければ、アプリは成功しません。Appストアは検索エンジンと同様に機能します。可視性のために最適化してください。

キーワード研究とターゲティング

高価値キーワードを見つける：

  • App AnnieやApp Radarなどのツールを使用
  • 検索ボリュームが多く、競争が少ないキーワードを特定
  • ロングテールキーワード（3～5単語）に焦点を当てる（達成可能）
  • アプリ名とサブタイトルでキーワードをテスト

例：

  • ジェネリック：「タスクマネージャー」（何百万ものアプリ、ランク不可能）
  • 特定：「フリーランサー向けシンプルな日々のタスクプランナー」（達成可能）

説得力のあるビジュアル要素

アプリアイコン：

  • 小さいサイズでも即座に認識可能
  • ブランドと一貫性
  • アプリストアのリストで競合他社のアイコンから目立つ

スクリーンショット：

  • 最初のスクリーンショットが最も重要です。40％のユーザーがここで決定します
  • コアバリューを2～3秒のビジュアルスキャンで表示
  • テキストオーバーレイを使用して利点を説明し、機能ではなく
  • ビフォー/アフタータイプスクリーンショットは優れた変換

プレビュービデオ：

  • 15～30秒の最大
  • 実際の問題を解決するアプリの動作を表示
  • アクセシビリティのためにテキストキャプションを含める

アプリの説明のベストプラクティス

構造：

  1. フック：アプリが何をするのか、誰のためのかを説明する1つの文
  2. 問題：アプリが何のフラストレーションや課題を解決するのか？
  3. ソリューション：どのようにあなたのアプリが異なるように解決しますか？
  4. 利点：ユーザーが経験する3つの具体的な成果は何ですか？
  5. 行動喚起：「今日無料ダウンロード」または「7日間無料で試す」

例フック：「美しいチャートであなたの毎日の習慣を追跡してください。改善したいが複雑なアプリを嫌う人のためにのアプリ。」

タイミングとソフトローンチ

プリローンチフェーズ：

  • ローンチ前にランディングページを構築
  • ベータテスターと友人にメールして初日にダウンロード
  • 早期ダウンロードはアルゴリズムランキングを劇的に向上
  • 最初の週のパフォーマンスは数ヶ月の発見を影響

ソフトローンチ戦略：

  • 小さな英語話す国（カナダ、UK、オーストラリア）でローンチ
  • 初期レビューとレーティングを収集
  • 発見された問題を修正
  • 改良版でグローバルにロールアウト

予算でのこのフェーズの成長戦略

アプリの構築は10％のコーディング、90％のユーザー取得です。広告の支出なしで成長する方法です。

有機的成長戦術

1. プレスとポッドキャスト

  • あなたのニッチのテック記者とポッドキャストホストを特定
  • パーソナライズされたピッチはしばしばカバレッジを取得します
  • 関連するポッドキャストの1つのメンション=500以上のダウンロード
  • コスト：良いピッチを書く時間

2. RedditとコミュニティEngagement

  • あなたのアプリが実際の問題を解決する場所をサブレディットを見つけ
  • 販売なしに真正に参加
  • 関連性があるときに関連性のあるときにアプリを共有
  • 昇進する前にコミュニティ信頼を構築

3. Product Huntローンチ

  • ローンチ日を戦略的に選択（火～水が最適に機能）
  • ローンチ日前にアウトリーチでオーディエンスを構築
  • ローンチ日のコメントと質問にアテンド
  • トップ5ランキング=1,000～3,000ダウンロード

4. コンテンツマーケティング

  • あなたのアプリが解決する問題についてブログ
  • あなたのアプリの機能に関連する詳細ガイドを作成
  • SEO最適化：あなたのオーディエンスが検索するキーワード
  • 関連する記事の中でアプリへのリンク

5. ソーシャルメディア構築

  • Twitter/Xであなたの開発の旅を共有
  • 学習と舞台裏を記録
  • ウイルスツイート、アプリ課題に関する関心ドライブ
  • TikTokとYouTube：「アプリのウォークスルー」ビデオドライブダウンロード

紹介プログラム

ユーザーが友人を招待するインセンティブを与える：

  • 3人の友人を紹介するための無料プレミアム月
  • 5人の招待に対して排他的な機能のロック解除
  • 組み込み共有機能により紹介がシームレスに
  • 各紹介されたユーザーは30～40％の有料顧客になる可能性があります

戦略的パートナーシップ

  • 補完的なアプリを特定し、クロスプロモーション達成
  • あなたのニッチのインフルエンサーはしばしば彼らがあなたのアプリを愛する場合は無料で昇進
  • 他のアプリとバンドル（AppSumoなど）
  • あなたのオーディエンスに関連するポッドキャストへのゲスト出演

成功事例：何が可能か

生産性アプリの例

ソロ開発者がタスク管理アプリを構築。フリーランサーのニッチをターゲット。

1年目：

  • フリーミアムモデルでローンチ
  • 5,000無料ユーザー；月額$7で100有料加入者
  • 収益：$8,400
  • 時間投資：8ヶ月で週20時間

2年目：

  • 25,000無料ユーザー；800有料加入者
  • 収益：$67,200
  • カスタマーサポート用の契約者を雇う
  • 純利益：$52,000

3年目：

  • 80,000無料ユーザー；2,500有料加入者
  • 収益：$210,000
  • アプリでフルタイムで仕事をすることができます
  • 利益：$150,000以上

ゲームの例

ソロ開発者がモバイルパズルゲームを作成。カジュアルなゲームプレイに焦点を当てる。

1年目：

  • 広告サポート：オプションプレミアムコンテンツ
  • 100,000ダウンロード；60,000月活動ユーザー
  • 平均ユーザーあたりの収益：月額$0.15
  • 月間収益：$9,000
  • 年間収益：$108,000

これは運ではなく、戦略的な設計、スマートマーケティング、継続された努力です。

時間管理：サイドプロジェクトとしてアプリを構築

ほとんどの成功したソロ開発者はすぐに仕事をやめませんでした。

パートタイム開発者スケジュール

週：

  • 月～金：日を仕事（9～5）
  • 平日の夜：アプリで1～2時間（午後7～9時）
  • 土曜日：4～5時間の焦点開発
  • 日曜日：計画、軽いコーディング、または管理

合計：週12～15時間は毎年1～2のアプリを確実に生成

現実的なタイムライン

  • MVP（最小実行可能製品）：集中的なRork時間の60～80時間
  • 最初の有料顧客：ローンチ後3～4ヶ月
  • 持続的な収入：12～18ヶ月
  • フルタイム収入：24～36ヶ月

パスは明確です：12～18ヶ月で週12～15時間が意味のあるサイド収入を取得します。

エネルギー管理

  • **深い仕事ブロック：**ピークエネルギー時間中にコードを実装
  • **管理タスク：**低エネルギー期間中にメール/マーケティング処理
  • **コンテキスト切り替え回避：**コーディングVSマーケティングに専用日
  • **同様のタスクをバッチ：**散在した取り組みより2時間のマーケティングスプリント

あなたの次のステップ

  1. **この週にアイデアを検証：**理想的なカスタマーを書き留めてください。彼らの最大のフラストレーションは何ですか？
  2. **2週間でプロトタイプを構築：**Rorkを使用してコアコンセプトをテスト
  3. **本物の人々とテスト：**ターゲット市場の10人と共有
  4. **マネタイズを決定：**どの戦略があなたのアプリの性質と一致していますか？
  5. **あなたのMVPを起動：**完璧より十分に優れている

アプリのビジネスはロトではなく、反復、カスタマーフィードバック、永続性を通じて洗練される工芸です。Rorkはあなたにツールを与えます。あなたの献身が結果を決定します。

Rorkでのソロデベロッパーはかつてないほど何か価値があるものを構築するオッズを持っていたことがありません。チーム、投資家、または何年ものコーディング経験が必要な時代は終わります。あなたの競争優位性は、ユニークなアイデア、学ぶことへの意思、実際の問題を解決することへのコミットメントです。

今日始めてください。あなたの利益が出るアプリビジネスはあなたを待っています。

en.title: Building Your First AI Companion App with React Native
en.author: Rork Lab
en.description: Learn how to build a simple AI chat companion app using Expo and React Native. Connect to Claude or OpenAI and create your own Replika-like experience.
en.content:
Building Your First AI Companion App with React Native

Have you ever wanted to create your own AI companion app? With Expo and React Native, you can build a chat interface that talks to an AI in just a few hours. In this guide, we'll create a simple but functional AI partner app from scratch.

What We're Building

We'll create a basic mobile app where users can chat with an AI companion. The app will:

  • Display chat messages in conversation bubbles
  • Send text to Claude or OpenAI's API
  • Persist conversations using AsyncStorage
  • Show a simple persona for the AI character

Think of it like a beginner's version of Replika or Character.ai—nothing fancy, but genuinely functional.

Project Setup

Start a new Expo project:

Install the dependencies we'll need:

Basic Chat UI Component

First, let's create the core chat interface. This is a ScrollView with chat bubbles:

Connecting to Claude API

Now let's add the AI API integration. Create a services/api.ts file:

Important: Handling API Keys Safely

Never hardcode API keys in your app! For production, you need a backend service. For now, this is just for learning. In the beginner phase, consider:

  • Creating a simple Node.js backend that proxies requests
  • Using environment variables with eas build
  • Never shipping API keys in your app binary

Persistence with AsyncStorage

We've already added AsyncStorage to save conversations. Users can close the app and their chat history will be there when they return:

Next Steps

You've built the core of an AI companion app! From here, you might want to:

  • Add a settings screen to change the AI persona/system prompt
  • Implement dark mode for a more polished feel
  • Add avatars to make the companion more visual
  • Create a "clear history" button for privacy

Important: AI Limitations & Honesty

Remember: your AI companion is just a language model. It doesn't truly understand emotions, have memory across sessions (unless you implement it), or feel affection. Be honest with your users about these limitations. This builds trust and sets healthy expectations.

Summary

You now have a working AI chat app with:

  • ✅ Clean chat UI with message bubbles
  • ✅ Claude API integration
  • ✅ Message persistence
  • ✅ Responsive keyboard handling

Deploy it to your phone with Expo Go and start chatting with your AI! Next, let's explore more advanced features.

en.title: Polished AI Companion Apps: UI, Memory & Personas
en.author: Rork Lab
en.description: Build a professional AI companion app with multiple personas, SQLite persistence, animations, and push notifications. Learn production-grade patterns from Replika and Character.ai.
en.content:
Polished AI Companion Apps: UI, Memory & Personas

You've built your first chat app. Now let's make it production-ready. We'll add multiple AI personas, persistent memory, smooth animations, and push notifications—just like Replika or Character.ai.

Architecture Overview

An intermediate companion app has several layers:

  1. UI Layer — Polished chat bubbles, typing indicators, avatars
  2. Storage Layer — SQLite for structured conversation history
  3. Memory System — Conversation summaries injected into prompts
  4. Persona System — Switch between different AI characters
  5. Notifications — Remind users to chat (with permission)

Let's build each piece.

Setting Up SQLite

First, install and initialize SQLite:

Create a database service:

Multiple Personas

Define different AI personalities:

Memory System with Summaries

Implement conversation memory by summarizing old messages:

Polished Chat UI with Typing Indicators

Create a refined chat component:

Persona Switching Screen

Allow users to switch between AI companions:

Push Notifications for Daily Check-ins

Add gentle reminders:

Dark Mode Support

Add theme support using Context:

Summary

Your intermediate AI companion app now has:

  • ✅ SQLite for structured data
  • ✅ Multiple AI personas with unique personalities
  • ✅ Conversation memory via summaries
  • ✅ Polished UI with typing indicators
  • ✅ Push notifications
  • ✅ Dark mode support

You're now at feature parity with simpler versions of Replika. Ready to take it to production? Let's explore the advanced guide next.

en.title: Production AI Companion Apps: Streaming, Voice & Monetization
en.author: Rork Lab

Production AI Companion Apps: Streaming, Voice & Monetization

Welcome to the advanced tier. We're building a production-ready AI companion that users will pay for. This means streaming responses, voice input/output, analytics, offline support, and proper monetization infrastructure.

Architecture for Scale

A production companion app has:

  1. Backend Service — Secure API key handling, request validation, rate limiting
  2. Streaming Engine — Real-time text display as the AI responds
  3. Voice Layer — Speech-to-text and text-to-speech
  4. Analytics — User behavior, retention, subscription metrics
  5. Monetization — RevenueCat for subscriptions
  6. Offline Mode — SQLite-based offline responses
  7. Security — Encrypted storage, privacy compliance

Backend with Cloudflare Workers

Never expose API keys in production. Use a serverless backend:

Streaming Responses in React Native

Display AI responses in real-time:

Voice Input & Output

Implement speech-to-text and text-to-speech:

Mood Tracking & Analytics

Add a mood journal feature:

Offline Support with Background Sync

Enable users to chat offline and sync when back online:

Subscriptions with RevenueCat

Implement in-app purchases:

App Store Submission Guidelines for AI Companions

Before submitting to the App Store, review Apple's policies:

Key requirements:

  • Clear disclosure that this is an AI (not a real person)
  • Privacy policy explaining data usage
  • Parental controls recommended for users under 18
  • No misleading health/mental health claims
  • Terms of Service explaining limitations
  • Proper handling of user data (encryption at rest/in transit)
  • Clear opt-in for notifications
  • Don't store payment info locally

Example privacy disclosure in your app:

Security Best Practices

  1. Encryption at Rest:
  1. API Key Rotation:
  • Rotate tokens server-side monthly
  • Invalidate old tokens after grace period
  • Monitor for unusual usage patterns
  1. Data Privacy:

Monitoring & Analytics

Track important metrics:

Summary: Production Checklist

  • ✅ Voice input/output
  • ✅ Mood tracking
  • ✅ Offline support

Your production AI companion is now ready to scale. Good luck shipping! 🚀

en.title: Rork × Gemini 2.0 Flash Complete Guide: How the AI Engine Works
en.author: Rork Lab
en.description: Discover how Rork's default AI engine—Gemini 2.0 Flash—powers autonomous app generation, and learn practical prompting techniques to build better apps faster.
en.content:
Rork is far more than a tool that converts prompts into code. Under the hood, a sophisticated AI agent built around Gemini 2.0 Flash autonomously architects, writes, and refines your entire mobile app. This guide breaks down how Rork's AI engine actually works—and shows you practical techniques to generate higher-quality apps, faster.

Why Rork Chose Gemini 2.0 Flash

Rork's internal AI engine (RorkAgent) runs on Google's Gemini 2.0 Flash as its default model. Here's why it beat out the competition:

1. Built for multi-turn tool calling

When you submit a prompt in Rork, an agentic loop fires off up to 11 tool calls behind the scenes—file creation, dependency resolution, preview builds, error detection, and more. Gemini 2.0 Flash was purpose-built for exactly this kind of high-frequency, sequential tool orchestration, delivering both speed and accuracy where it matters.

2. Excellent code generation quality

Generating React Native (Expo) code requires simultaneously reasoning about component architecture, navigation patterns, and state management. Gemini 2.0 Flash has been fine-tuned on the Expo/React Native ecosystem, making it a natural fit for Rork's core output.

3. Cost-performance at scale

Gemini 2.0 Flash offers some of the most competitive token pricing in its class. This lets Rork serve hundreds of thousands of users with real-time app generation while keeping subscription costs reasonable—a win that passes directly to you.

Inside RorkAgent: The 11-Step Agentic Loop

When you type a prompt into Rork, here's what actually happens behind the scenes:

Your prompt
  ↓
RorkAgent (Gemini 2.0 Flash)
  ↓
[Agentic loop — up to 11 turns]
  1. Design the file structure
  2. Generate package.json (resolve dependencies)
  3. Create App.tsx / entry point
  4. Define navigation structure
  5. Generate screen components
  6. Implement state management logic
  7. Add API integration code
  8. Apply stylesheets
  9. Run error checks and auto-fix
  10. Generate preview build
  11. Final code review
  ↓
Finished React Native (Expo) app

This entire process typically completes in seconds to a few minutes. You say "build me a task manager app," and Gemini 2.0 Flash handles everything from architecture to implementation.

Gemini 2.0 Flash vs. Claude: When to Use Which in Rork

Rork's engine supports both GeminiProvider and ClaudeProvider (Rork Max leverages Claude Opus for native Swift apps). Here's a practical comparison:

| Dimension | Gemini 2.0 Flash | Claude | |-----------|-----------------|--------| | Speed | Extremely fast | Medium–fast | | Code generation accuracy | High | Very high | | Context window | Up to 1M tokens | Up to 200K tokens | | Best for | Rapid prototyping, iterative dev | Complex logic, high-fidelity implementation | | In Rork | Default (Standard plan) | Used in Rork Max |

Use Gemini 2.0 Flash when you need to iterate quickly on ideas or build straightforward apps. Switch to Rork Max (Claude Opus) when your app involves complex business logic, native Swift capabilities, or strict security requirements.

For a deeper look at Rork Max's AI capabilities, see Rork Max AI Features Complete Guide.

Prompting Techniques That Get the Best from Gemini 2.0 Flash

Getting great results from Rork is largely about prompting well. Gemini 2.0 Flash excels at multi-turn context—so giving it clear structure from the start dramatically improves output quality.

1. Spell out your screen structure upfront

Bad:

Make me a chat app

Good:

Build a chat app with these 3 screens:
1. Login screen (email + password, Supabase auth)
2. Conversation list screen (list of chats, button to start new conversation)
3. Chat detail screen (message feed + send form, real-time updates via Supabase)

Gemini 2.0 Flash's strong multi-turn comprehension means an explicit screen structure at the start dramatically sharpens the agentic loop.

2. Name your tech stack explicitly

Use Expo Router v3 for navigation,
Zustand for state management,
and Supabase (PostgreSQL) for the database.
Build me a task management app.

Gemini will match its output to your specified stack—saving you a round of post-generation edits.

3. Describe your design intent in words

Minimalist design with dark mode support.
Base color: dark grey (#1a1a2e), accent: purple (#6c63ff).
Use system fonts throughout.

Gemini 2.0 Flash translates natural-language design descriptions directly into StyleSheet definitions with impressive fidelity.

4. Lean on Fix Now for errors

When generated code throws an error, hit Fix Now. Gemini 2.0 Flash analyzes the error message, pinpoints the root cause, and regenerates the affected code—often adding null checks and fallback values automatically.

Error: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'map')

→ Click Fix Now
→ Gemini identifies undefined state before data loads
→ Auto-adds null check and empty array fallback
→ Fixed ✓

See How to Use Fix Now Auto-Debug for a full walkthrough.

Practical Example: Building a TODO App with Gemini 2.0 Flash

Below is a representative sample of the React Native code Rork generates via Gemini 2.0 Flash. Notice the TypeScript types, clean component structure, and in-line comments—all added automatically.

Expected output: A dark-themed (#1a1a2e) TODO list screen where users can type a task and tap "Add" to append it to the list. Tapping the circle toggles completion (completed items show a strikethrough). Tapping "Delete" removes the item. When empty, the screen shows "No tasks yet."

Leveraging Gemini's Long Context for Iterative Development

One of Gemini 2.0 Flash's standout capabilities is its 1-million-token context window. Combined with Rork's conversational development style, this becomes a genuine superpower.

Build features incrementally without losing context

Turn 1: "Add user authentication with Supabase"
Turn 2: "Using that auth, build a profile screen"
Turn 3: "Add image upload to the profile screen using Supabase Storage"

Gemini retains the full context of your project across all turns, ensuring new features integrate cleanly with existing code rather than creating conflicts.

Request project-wide refactoring

"Look at the whole project and extract any duplicated logic into custom hooks.
In particular, consolidate all auth-related code into a useAuth hook."

Gemini 2.0 Flash's long-context understanding lets it reason about your entire codebase at once—making coherent refactoring suggestions that a narrow-context model would miss.

How Gemini and Claude Collaborate in Rork Max

In Rork Max, Gemini 2.0 Flash and Claude Opus play complementary roles:

  • Gemini 2.0 Flash: Rapid prototyping, iterative fixes, error detection
  • Claude Opus: Complex business logic, native Swift code generation, security-critical implementations

For authentication flows, HealthKit integrations, or Siri Intents—where precision matters most—Claude Opus takes the lead automatically. This handoff is invisible to users; Rork manages it behind the scenes.

FAQ

Q: Can I choose which AI model Rork uses?

A: As of March 2026, the Standard plan automatically uses Gemini 2.0 Flash. To access Claude, you'll need to upgrade to Rork Max. Model selection for Standard plan users may be added in a future update. See Rork Pricing Plans Explained for the latest plan details.

Q: What should I do when Gemini 2.0 Flash doesn't generate what I expected?

A: Try these steps: (1) Make your prompt more specific—add the number of screens, preferred tech stack, and design guidance. (2) Use Fix Now to auto-correct errors. (3) Build incrementally—add one feature at a time rather than describing everything upfront. Smaller, focused requests consistently outperform large, vague ones.

Q: Is Gemini 2.0 Flash-generated code production-ready?

A: Yes. Rork generates standard Expo/React Native TypeScript code that's fully compatible with App Store and Google Play publishing. That said, always review generated code before shipping—especially anything involving API keys, authentication tokens, or user data. Security-sensitive logic deserves a manual audit regardless of how it was written.

Q: What makes Rork's Gemini integration different from other AI app builders?

A: Rork is purpose-built for mobile apps. While tools like Bolt or Lovable are optimized for web, Rork's entire prompting pipeline, tool set, and context management are tuned for the Expo/React Native ecosystem. The same Gemini 2.0 Flash model produces very different results depending on how it's orchestrated—and Rork's mobile-first orchestration is what sets it apart.

Q: Does Gemini 2.0 Flash handle prompts in languages other than English?

A: Yes, fully. You can write prompts in Japanese, Spanish, or any other major language, and Gemini will interpret them accurately. Note that generated code comments default to English—if you need Japanese comments, simply add "write all code comments in Japanese" to your prompt.

Wrap-Up: Use Rork Smarter by Understanding Its AI Engine

Rork chose Gemini 2.0 Flash for three reasons: the speed of its multi-turn tool calling, the quality of its code generation, and its cost efficiency at scale. Understanding this gives you a real edge—structured prompts, explicit tech stack choices, and incremental feature additions all play to Gemini's strengths.

The Rork × Gemini 2.0 Flash combination has made mobile app development accessible to solo developers, designers, and founders who couldn't write native code before. Start experimenting today and see how far a well-crafted prompt can take you.

For more on getting the most from AI-powered development, check out AI Code Generation Workflow: A Complete Guide.

en.title: Build Apple Vision Pro Apps with Rork Max — Complete visionOS Guide
en.author: Rork Lab
en.description: Learn how to build native visionOS apps for Apple Vision Pro using only natural language with Rork Max. Cover spatial UI, hand gestures, 3D content, and App Store publishing — no Xcode or Swift knowledge required.
en.content:
Spatial Computing, Simplified

Apple Vision Pro introduced a fundamentally new paradigm: spatial computing. Eye tracking, hand gestures, immersive 3D environments — building apps that leverage these capabilities traditionally required deep expertise in SwiftUI, RealityKit, ARKit, and Xcode.

Rork Max removes that barrier entirely. You can now create and publish native visionOS apps using nothing but natural language prompts. This guide walks you through the complete process.

Understanding visionOS UI Concepts

Before diving in, familiarize yourself with visionOS-specific UI primitives that Rork Max will work with:

Window: A 2D content panel that floats in the user's space — similar to a regular app window but positioned in mixed reality.

Volume: A bounded 3D container for displaying three-dimensional content anchored in the real world.

Immersive Space: A full-environment experience that replaces the user's surroundings. Ideal for games, virtual tours, and deep-focus experiences.

Ornament: An accessory UI attached to the edge of a window — great for toolbars and supplemental controls.

Rork Max understands all of these constructs and selects the appropriate structure based on your natural language description.

Prerequisites

  • Rork Max subscription (Pro or higher recommended)
  • Apple Developer Program membership (required for App Store publishing)
  • Apple Vision Pro device or visionOS Simulator (via Xcode on Mac)
  • An app idea to bring to life

Step 1: Create a Vision Pro Project

Open Rork Max and create a new project. Specify Vision Pro as your platform in the prompt or settings panel.

Project name: SolarSystemVR
Platform: Apple Vision Pro (visionOS)
Description: An educational app where users explore the solar system in 3D.
Display all planets in an immersive space.
When a planet is pinch-tapped, show a detail window with facts and stats.
Planets orbit the sun at realistic relative speeds.

From this single prompt, Rork Max generates:

  • An ImmersiveSpace with a RealityKit 3D scene
  • Orbiting planet objects with animation
  • Pinch gesture interaction handlers
  • Planet detail Windows with facts content
  • SwiftUI main menu interface

Step 2: Design the Spatial UI

Depth and dimensionality are critical to a great Vision Pro experience. Refine the UI through follow-up prompts:

Update the main menu:
1. Display the title "Solar System VR" as extruded 3D text
2. Show a planet list as horizontally scrollable glass-morphism cards
3. Each card should show a rotating 3D mini planet
4. Animate a zoom-in effect when a card is selected

Rork Max is trained on Apple's visionOS Human Interface Guidelines and automatically handles appropriate depth, sizing, and contrast for spatial interfaces.

Step 3: Configure Hand Gestures and Eye Tracking

Unlock the full interaction model of Vision Pro:

Add the following interactions:
1. Pinch and drag with both hands to reposition the solar system
2. Gaze hover on a planet highlights it and shows its name
3. Spread pinch = zoom in, close pinch = zoom out
4. Palm-up gesture opens the main menu

Rork Max implements these using native visionOS APIs (ARKit + RealityKit) — no framework knowledge required on your end.

Step 4: Add 3D Content and Visual Effects

Make the experience visually stunning:

Enhance the 3D content:
1. Add a corona particle effect to the Sun
2. Show cloud animations and city lights on the night side of Earth
3. Animate Jupiter's Great Red Spot
4. Apply realistic ring textures to Saturn
5. Use real star catalog data for the background skybox

Rork Max includes a built-in 3D asset library. Common objects — planets, vehicles, nature, furniture — are available instantly. You can also upload custom USDZ models from Reality Composer or Blender.

Step 5: Customize Immersion Levels

Allow users to choose how immersed they want to be:

Add a settings panel as an Ornament:
- Immersion level slider (Mixed ←→ Full Immersive)
- Movement speed adjustment
- Ambient space sound toggle
- Language switcher (English / Japanese)

For deeper AR experiences using ARKit and LiDAR, see our ARKit guide.

Step 6: Testing with the Simulator

No Vision Pro hardware? No problem.

Rork Max Built-in Preview: Validate UI layout and flow with text/image mockups.

visionOS Simulator: Export the generated code from Rork Max → open in Xcode → run on the Vision Pro Simulator. Mouse clicks simulate tap interactions; the simulator renders the full spatial environment.

Common errors and how to handle them:

If you see: "ImmersiveSpaceError - cannot present while another space is active"

Tell Rork Max: "Fix the immersive space state management error.
Ensure only one immersive space is active at a time."

The "Fix Now" button in Rork Max will also auto-diagnose and resolve most build errors. Learn more in our Fix Now auto-debug guide.

Step 7: Publish to the App Store

Vision Pro app publishing follows the same flow as iPhone — made even simpler by Rork Max.

  1. Click "Publish" in Rork Max — automatic build and archive
  2. Connect Apple Developer Account (first time only)
  3. Fill in App Store Connect metadata
    • App name and description (bilingual recommended)
    • Screenshots (Vision Pro requires special capture dimensions)
    • Age rating and category
  4. Optional: TestFlight beta — distribute to testers for feedback
  5. Submit for review — Apple typically responds within 1–3 days

Important for Vision Pro apps: Apple pays close attention to how eye-tracking and hand-tracking data is used. Your privacy policy must clearly describe any data collection. Rork Max auto-generates a privacy policy template that covers these requirements.

Best Practices for visionOS Development

Design for Depth

The z-axis is your new dimension. Establish a clear depth hierarchy:

Optimize depth placement:
- Main content: 70cm in front of user
- Secondary menus: 1m in front
- Environmental background: 3m+
- All text labels: Billboard-facing (always facing the user)

Performance Optimization

Vision Pro renders at high refresh rates across a wide field of view. Optimize aggressively:

Optimize rendering performance:
- Skip rendering planets outside the view frustum (frustum culling)
- Use low-poly LOD models for distant planets
- Limit shadows to nearby objects only
- Maintain 60fps+ across all interactions

Accessibility

Not all users have the same physical capabilities:

Add accessibility support:
- VoiceOver labels for all planets (with descriptive text)
- Dynamic Type support for all text elements
- Fallback to hand-tap interactions for users with eye-tracking limitations

For multi-platform deployment across iPhone, iPad, and Vision Pro, see the Rork Max Apple Multi-Platform Guide.

Frequently Asked Questions

Do I need a physical Vision Pro to develop?

No. The visionOS Simulator on Mac lets you build and test without hardware. Mouse input substitutes for hand gestures, and the simulator renders the full spatial environment. For complete gesture and eye-tracking testing, a physical device is ideal — but you can ship a polished app without one.

Can I port my existing iPhone app to Vision Pro?

Yes. Tell Rork Max: "Adapt this iPhone app for Vision Pro. Use a Window layout and replace tap interactions with pinch gestures." Rork Max identifies incompatible APIs and suggests visionOS-compatible alternatives. Full one-shot porting isn't guaranteed for complex apps, but the AI dramatically reduces migration time.

Is App Store review stricter for Vision Pro apps?

The core review process is the same, but Vision Pro apps face additional scrutiny around privacy — specifically eye-tracking and hand-tracking data. You must clearly disclose how this data is handled. Also avoid keeping ImmersiveSpace active unnecessarily, as Apple may flag this as a poor user experience. Rork Max follows Apple's best practices by default.

Do I need to supply my own 3D models?

No. Rork Max includes an asset library covering common objects — just describe what you need. For custom models, upload USDZ files created with Reality Composer, Blender, or any compatible tool. The AI integrates them into your scene automatically.

Can I integrate Siri with my Vision Pro app?

Yes — using App Intents. Just tell Rork Max: "Add a Siri shortcut that opens the immersive space when the user says 'Explore planets'." The AI handles the App Intents implementation. See the Rork Max Siri & App Intents guide for more.

Takeaway

Building for Apple Vision Pro used to require months of specialized learning. With Rork Max, you can go from idea to published visionOS app using nothing but natural language — no Swift, no RealityKit expertise, no Xcode configuration.

Spatial computing is still in its early days. The developers who build now will define what Vision Pro experiences look like for the next decade. Start with a simple concept, prompt Rork Max, and ship something spatial today.

en.title: Building 3D Games with Rork Max: A Complete Guide to Metal Framework and AI-Assisted Game Development
en.author: Rork Lab
en.description: Learn how to build native 3D iOS games using Rork Max and Apple's Metal framework—no Swift expertise required. Covers game loops, physics, scene setup, and Metal shaders with AI assistance.
en.content:
Introduction: AI Opens the Door to Game Development

Until recently, building native 3D games for iOS meant mastering Swift, understanding Apple's Metal graphics pipeline, and investing months of development time. Rork Max changes this completely—you can now build real, shippable 3D games simply by describing what you want to an AI.

This guide walks you through leveraging Rork Max's cloud Mac compilation environment and Apple's Metal framework to create polished 3D games. Whether you're dreaming of a Pokémon GO-inspired experience or a fast-paced 3D action game, let's turn your vision into reality.

What You'll Learn

  • How Rork Max enables full 3D game development through native Swift
  • The basics of Apple's Metal framework and game loop architecture
  • Step-by-step prompts for building a working 3D game
  • Common errors and how to fix them
  • How to monetize and publish your game

Who This Guide Is For

  • Developers new to Rork Max looking to push its limits
  • Creators who hit React Native's walls when pursuing 3D graphics
  • Solo developers who want to ship a game app without deep Swift knowledge

What Is Metal, and Why Does Rork Max Make It Powerful?

Apple Metal at a Glance

Metal is Apple's low-level graphics API that gives developers direct access to the GPU. It powers everything from 3D games to machine learning acceleration across iOS, macOS, and tvOS. Compared to the deprecated OpenGL ES, Metal delivers significantly lower CPU overhead and dramatically better performance.

Key Metal capabilities include:

  • Minimal overhead: Reduces draw call costs by minimizing CPU-GPU communication
  • Multithreaded rendering: Encode GPU commands from multiple threads simultaneously
  • Metal Shading Language (MSL): A C++-based language for writing custom GPU programs
  • RealityKit integration: Seamlessly connects with Apple's AR and photorealistic rendering framework

Why You Need Rork Max for This

Standard Rork (React Native) cannot access Metal directly. The JavaScript bridge architecture fundamentally limits low-level GPU operations.

Rork Max solves this from the ground up. By compiling native Swift on a cloud fleet of Macs, Rork Max unlocks:

  • Full Metal API access for GPU-level graphics programming
  • SceneKit and RealityKit for high-level 3D scene management
  • Game Controller support (MFi controllers, game pads)
  • SpriteKit for 2.5D game experiences

With Rork Max, the entire iOS game development stack is available through AI-guided prompting.

Setup: What You Need Before You Start

Requirements

  1. Rork Max plan — Metal and 3D game development require Rork Max ($200/month). Standard and free plans only generate React Native code.
  2. Apple Developer Account — Required for App Store submission and device testing ($99/year).
  3. iPhone or iPad for testing — Rork's browser simulator handles basic checks, but Metal performance must be validated on real hardware.
  4. TestFlight or QR code access — Rork Max generates a QR code or TestFlight link to install builds directly on your device.

Creating a Rork Max Project

From the Rork dashboard, click "New Project" and select "Native (Rork Max)" as the project type. Rork Max projects are distinct from standard Rork projects—always choose Max when building Metal-based experiences.

Building a 3D Game Step by Step

Step 1: Describe Your Game Concept

All Rork Max interaction happens in natural language. Be specific about the mechanics you want—the more detail you give, the closer the first generation will be to your vision.

Example prompt:
"Create a 3D ball-rolling game using SceneKit with these features:
 - Control the ball using the device tilt sensor (CoreMotion)
 - The floor has holes—falling through is game over
 - Collect coins to increase your score
 - 60-second time limit
 - Include a title screen and game over screen"

Rork Max will generate a complete, runnable SceneKit-based project from this single prompt.

Step 2: Review the Generated Code

Rork Max produces structured Swift code you can inspect in the editor. Here's a representative sample of the game controller:

The physics engine handles gravity and collision automatically—you don't need to write a single line of physics math.

Step 3: Iterate with Refinement Prompts

After the initial generation, refine your game iteratively:

"Make the coins sparkle with a continuous rotation animation.
 Add a particle burst effect when the ball falls into a hole.
 Include a looping 8-bit style background music track."

Rork Max will integrate SCNParticleSystem for the burst effect and AVAudioEngine for the soundtrack.

Step 4: Add Metal Shaders for Visual Impact

For richer visuals, request custom Metal shaders:

"Add a glow effect to the ball using a Metal shader.
 When the score exceeds 10, the glow intensity should increase
 to give visual feedback for high-score progress."

Common Errors and How to Fix Them

Error 1: "SCNScene file not found"

Symptom: Crash on launch with a missing GameScene.scn error.

Fix: Ask Rork Max to build the scene programmatically instead of from a file:

"Change the scene setup to build the environment entirely in code
 using SCNScene() and SCNNode() instead of loading a .scn file."

Error 2: Physics not working — ball floats in place

Symptom: The ball ignores gravity and stays suspended.

Fix: The physics world gravity may not be configured:

"Set gameScene.physicsWorld.gravity to SCNVector3(0, -9.8, 0)
 and ensure all floor nodes have static physics bodies."

Error 3: Tilt controls unresponsive

Symptom: Tilting the device has no effect on ball movement.

Fix: CoreMotion doesn't work in the simulator—test on a real device. Also add a fallback:

"Add a tap-to-move fallback UI when running in the simulator
 so CoreMotion is only activated on real hardware."

Error 4: Frame rate drops during gameplay

Symptom: Noticeable stuttering as more objects appear.

Fix: Optimize scene rendering:

"Set preferredFramesPerSecond to 60 on SCNView.
 Implement Level of Detail (LOD) so distant objects
 use lower-polygon versions automatically."

Advanced: Taking Your Game Further

Game Center Leaderboards

Rork Max can integrate Apple's Game Center for competitive features:

"Add a Game Center leaderboard called 'High Scores'.
 Submit the player's score to GKLeaderboard when the game ends,
 and show a leaderboard button on the title screen."

Upgrading to RealityKit for Photorealistic Visuals

For AAA-quality visuals, consider moving to RealityKit:

"Migrate the game from SceneKit to RealityKit using the
 Entity Component System (ECS) architecture.
 Enable physically-based rendering and dynamic shadows."

Monetization

Once your game is polished, add in-app purchases using Rork Max + RevenueCat:

"Add in-app purchases using RevenueCat.
 Implement two coin packs: 100 coins for $0.99 and
 500 coins for $3.99. Show the purchase UI after game over."

For publishing to the App Store, see our iOS App Publishing Guide.

FAQ

Q1. Can I build a 3D game with Rork Max even with zero game development experience?

A. Yes—for simpler games, absolutely. Rork Max handles the low-level implementation. That said, having a basic understanding of concepts like physics bodies, game loops, and scene graphs will help you write more effective prompts and debug issues faster. Start simple and layer in complexity incrementally.

Q2. Are Metal shaders fully supported in Rork Max?

A. Yes. Rork Max can generate Metal Shading Language (MSL) code and apply it to SceneKit or RealityKit materials. Describe the visual effect you want, and Rork Max will write the shader and wire it up. Highly complex, multi-pass shader pipelines may require a few rounds of refinement prompts.

Q3. Do I need special App Store approval to publish a Metal game?

A. No special process is required beyond standard App Store Review Guidelines. If your game includes in-app purchases or content for children, ensure you follow Apple's guidelines for those categories. Rork Max also assists with generating App Store metadata including screenshots and descriptions.

Q4. Can I test Metal rendering in the browser simulator?

A. You can test basic game logic and UI in Rork's browser simulator, but for accurate Metal performance and CoreMotion tilt controls, a real device is essential. Use the TestFlight link or QR code that Rork Max generates to install builds on your iPhone or iPad.

Q5. Can I port an existing Unity or Godot game to Rork Max?

A. There's no direct import path. However, you can describe your game's mechanics and Rork Max will re-implement them in native Swift. For small-to-medium projects this is practical; for larger games, consider a phased migration where you rebuild core gameplay first, then add secondary features.

Conclusion: AI Makes Native Game Development Accessible

Rork Max removes the biggest barriers to iOS game development—Swift expertise, Metal knowledge, and compilation infrastructure—by handling them through AI. You focus on the game design; Rork Max handles the implementation.

Key takeaways from this guide:

  • Metal is fully accessible in Rork Max's native Swift environment, unlike React Native-based Rork
  • SceneKit provides everything you need for 3D scenes, physics, and particle effects—all configurable through natural language prompts
  • Common errors have straightforward prompt-based fixes
  • Game Center, RealityKit, and in-app purchases extend your game into a competitive, monetized product

Ready to go further? Explore Rork Max AR and LiDAR development for immersive real-world experiences, or check out our in-app purchase guide to monetize your creation from day one.

en.title: Complete Guide to Push Notifications in Rork | Expo Notifications + Backend Integration
en.author: Rork Lab
en.description: Learn how to implement push notifications in your Rork-generated app step by step. From Expo Notifications setup and token registration to sending notifications from a Supabase or Node.js backend—with full code examples.
en.content:
Push notifications are one of the most effective tools for keeping users engaged with your app. Whether you're alerting users about new messages, announcing a sale, or reminding them of upcoming deadlines, notifications bring people back. This guide walks you through implementing push notifications in a Rork-generated app using Expo Notifications—from device token registration to backend delivery.

How Push Notifications Work with Rork

All apps generated by Rork are built on Expo (React Native). Expo provides the expo-notifications library, which gives you a unified API for both iOS (APNs) and Android (FCM) push notifications.

Here's the end-to-end flow:

App launches
  ↓
expo-notifications requests permission & retrieves device token
  ↓
Token saved to backend (Supabase, etc.)
  ↓
Server calls Expo Push API with token + message
  ↓
Expo Push Service forwards to APNs / FCM
  ↓
Notification delivered to user's device

Using the Expo Push Service means you don't have to manage APNs certificates or FCM credentials yourself—Expo handles the relay, making this approach ideal for solo developers and small teams.

Step 1: Set Up expo-notifications

Send Rork a prompt like this to scaffold the entire setup automatically:

Implement push notifications using expo-notifications.
Retrieve the device token and log it to the console.
Show an alert when a notification is received.

Rork will update app.json, install the necessary packages, and generate the notification handler. The resulting hook looks like this:

Expected behavior:

  • A permission dialog appears when the app first launches
  • After granting permission, an ExponentPushToken[...] is logged to the console
  • Notifications received while the app is open trigger a banner and sound

Step 2: Save the Token to Your Backend

The token must be stored on your backend so you can send notifications later. Here's how to do it with Supabase:

Set up a user_push_tokens table in Supabase with columns: user_id (text), expo_push_token (text), and updated_at (timestamptz). You can prompt Rork with "Create a user_push_tokens table in Supabase and save the user's Expo push token when they log in" to have it generate both the schema and the saving logic automatically.

For more on Supabase integration patterns, see the Rork × Supabase Chat App Guide.

Step 3: Send Notifications from Your Backend

With tokens stored, you're ready to send notifications. The Expo Push API accepts standard HTTP POST requests, making it easy to call from a Supabase Edge Function, Node.js server, or any HTTP-capable environment:

Once deployed, this Edge Function can be triggered by user actions, scheduled jobs, or webhooks to send targeted notifications at exactly the right moment.

Step 4: Deep Link to a Screen on Notification Tap

When a user taps a notification, you can navigate them directly to a relevant screen using the data field:

Set data: { screen: '/messages/123' } when sending the notification, and tapping it will route the user straight to that conversation. For tips on debugging navigation issues, check the Fix Now Auto-Debug Guide.

Push Notifications with Rork Max

Rork Max unlocks more advanced notification capabilities:

  • Rich notifications: Add images, video previews, and custom action buttons (iOS 15+ / Android 12+)
  • Silent notifications: Trigger background data sync without displaying a visible alert (content-available: 1)
  • Scheduled local notifications: Deliver reminders at specific times or on repeating patterns

Because Rork Max uses Claude Opus for native API work, you can prompt it with detailed requirements like "Add Reply and Mark as Read action buttons to iOS rich notifications" and get production-ready code in one pass.

FAQ

Q. I can't get a push token in the simulator. What's wrong?

A. Expo Push Token retrieval requires a physical device. On simulators and emulators, Device.isDevice returns false, so token registration is skipped by design. Test on a real device running Expo Go or an EAS Development Build.

Q. Push notifications aren't being delivered on iOS.

A. Check the following in order: (1) the ios.bundleIdentifier in app.json matches the App ID registered in your Apple Developer account; (2) you're using an EAS Build—not Expo Go—for production push delivery; (3) your EAS project is linked to the correct Apple team. Expo Go uses a shared certificate and does not support custom APNs delivery for your app's bundle ID.

Q. How do I send a broadcast notification to all users?

A. The Expo Push API accepts up to 100 tokens per request as an array. For large-scale broadcasts, split your token list into chunks of 100 and send batched requests. A Supabase pg_cron job or a queue-based worker is a clean way to handle this at scale. For higher engagement, consider segmenting by user attributes and personalizing the message body.

Q. How do I handle expired or invalid tokens?

A. When a user uninstalls and reinstalls your app, or revokes notification permissions, their old token becomes invalid. The Expo Push API returns a DeviceNotRegistered error for stale tokens. Add a cleanup step in your backend that deletes any token returning this error, preventing unnecessary API calls and keeping your token table clean.

Summary

Implementing push notifications in a Rork app comes down to four steps:

  1. Use expo-notifications to request permission and retrieve the device token
  2. Save the token to your backend (e.g., Supabase)
  3. Call the Expo Push API from a server to send notifications
  4. Use data.screen to deep-link users to the right screen on tap

You can prompt Rork with "Add push notifications, save the token to Supabase, and send from a backend Edge Function" to generate the majority of this code automatically. From there, it's just a matter of plugging in your project IDs, table names, and screen paths.

Push notifications, done right, are one of the highest-ROI features you can add to any mobile app. Build them in, and watch your retention numbers climb.

en.title: How to Implement Siri & App Intents with Rork Max
en.author: Rork Lab
en.description: Learn how to add Siri voice control and App Intents to your iOS app using Rork Max. Includes step-by-step prompts, Swift code examples, and widget integration for a task manager app.
en.content:
Why App Intents — and Why Rork Max?

"Hey Siri, add a task to my app." "Siri, what's on my list today?" Voice control through Siri is one of the most underused differentiators in the App Store. Users who can interact with your app without picking up their phone are measurably more engaged — yet most indie apps skip the integration because the implementation overhead has traditionally been high.

That overhead disappeared with App Intents (iOS 16+), Apple's modern Swift-native framework that connects your app to Siri, the Shortcuts app, Spotlight, Focus Filters, and home screen widget buttons. The catch: App Intents require native Swift. React Native wrappers exist, but they're brittle and lag behind the framework.

Rork Max generates native Swift, which means App Intents are first-class. Describe the voice commands you want, and Rork Max scaffolds the entire conformance — AppIntent structs, AppShortcutsProvider, parameter definitions, localized dialog strings, and error handling — in one focused prompt session.

This guide walks through a complete implementation using a task management app as the example. The same patterns apply to fitness apps, e-commerce, timers, or any app that benefits from voice-driven actions.

App Intents Architecture

The Building Blocks

Every App Intent is a Swift struct conforming to the AppIntent protocol with three parts:

| Component | Purpose | |-----------|---------| | title | Displayed in Siri, Shortcuts app, and Spotlight | | @Parameter | Inputs Siri collects from the user via voice | | perform() | Async function that runs your app logic and returns a Siri dialog |

When a user says "Hey Siri, add a task to [Your App]," the system maps the phrase to your AppShortcutsProvider, determines the matching intent, collects any missing parameters through follow-up questions, then calls perform().

What App Intents Connect To

Unlike the older SiriKit framework — which was limited to a fixed set of domains like messaging and payments — App Intents work for any app feature you choose to expose:

  • Siri — voice-triggered on device or via AirPods
  • Shortcuts app — users build personal automations around your intents
  • Spotlight — tap an intent directly from iOS search results
  • Widget buttons (iOS 17+) — home screen buttons that run an intent without opening the app
  • Apple Watch — intents work across the Apple ecosystem automatically

Building App Intents with Rork Max

Step 1: Prompt for the Full Intent Set

Create a new Rork Max project and provide a detailed opening prompt:

Rork Max will generate:

  • TaskAppIntents.swift — all three AppIntent structs
  • TaskShortcuts.swift — AppShortcutsProvider with Siri phrases
  • Updated TaskRepository — the data layer wired to the intents

Step 2: Review the Generated Intent Code

The core of what Rork Max produces:

Step 3: Register Siri Phrases

Provide the AppShortcutsProvider to define the specific phrases Siri recognizes:

\(.applicationName) is a special placeholder that expands to your app's name at runtime, ensuring Siri anchors phrases to your specific app.

Adding Widget Buttons (iOS 17+)

App Intents become even more powerful when paired with home screen widget buttons. Add this to your Rork Max session:

The generated widget view:

Common Errors and Fixes

Siri says "I can't do that in this app"

Your phrase isn't registered in AppShortcutsProvider, or the registered phrase doesn't closely match what you said. Prompt Rork Max: "Add more natural language variations to the Siri phrases in AppShortcutsProvider."

perform() runs but Siri shows a generic error

The error thrown doesn't conform to CustomLocalizedStringResourceConvertible. Add the conformance so Siri can vocalize a helpful message instead of a raw error string.

Widget button does nothing when tapped

Widget App Intent buttons require iOS 17+. On iOS 16, prompt Rork Max: "Fall back to a deep link for the widget button on iOS 16."

Intent crashes when accessing Core Data

App Intent extensions run in a separate process. Shared Core Data requires App Groups. Prompt: "Configure App Groups so the App Intent extension can access the shared Core Data store."

Use Case Prompts for Other App Types

Fitness App

Shopping App

Pomodoro Timer

FAQ

Do App Intents work on iPad and Mac?

Yes. App Intents run natively on iPhone, iPad, and Mac (via Mac Catalyst or native macOS, iOS 16+ / macOS 13+). Apple Watch is also supported, so a single intent definition reaches every device in the user's ecosystem.

Can I use App Intents with the standard Rork (React Native) version?

React Native community packages exist for limited SiriKit support, but full App Intents — especially widget button integration, Focus Filters, and custom parameter types — require native Swift. Rork Max is the right choice for a complete implementation.

How do I test App Intents in Rork Max?

Use the Shortcuts app on a simulator or real device: tap the "+" button, search for your app's name, and you'll see your registered intents. Trigger them directly in the Shortcuts app before testing via Siri voice. Rork Max's streaming simulator also supports Shortcuts app testing.

Can App Intents gate features behind a subscription?

Yes. Inside perform(), check the user's subscription state. If they're not subscribed, throw an error with a message like "Upgrade to Premium to use voice commands." Avoid launching a purchase sheet from within the intent — instead, return a deep link that opens the paywall in the app.

How many Siri phrases can I register per intent?

Apple recommends 3–5 phrases per intent. Each phrase should cover a different natural way to phrase the request. The \(.applicationName) placeholder is required in at least one phrase per intent per Apple's guidelines.

Summary

App Intents unlock a layer of engagement that most apps leave on the table. Users who can say "Hey Siri, add a task" or tap a widget button without opening your app return more often and rate the experience higher.

With Rork Max, you describe the voice commands you want and the framework is generated for you. The key principles:

  1. Keep each intent focused on one atomic action
  2. Use descriptive @Parameter titles so Siri asks the right follow-up questions
  3. Register 3–5 natural Siri phrases per intent in AppShortcutsProvider
  4. Return CustomLocalizedStringResourceConvertible errors for Siri-readable failure messages
  5. Add iOS 17 widget buttons for zero-tap access from the Home Screen

For a broader view of what's possible with native Apple APIs in Rork Max, see Using Native Apple APIs with Rork Max. To build the full iOS engagement stack — Live Activities, Widgets, and Siri — pair this guide with Home Screen & Lock Screen Widget Guide and Live Activities & Dynamic Island Guide.

en.title: Getting Started with Latest Performance Optimization Techniques in Expo SDK 55
en.author: Rork Lab
en.description: Comprehensive guide to Expo SDK 55's Hermes V1, EAS build caching, and modal improvements. Learn the latest performance optimization techniques with implementation examples.
en.content:
Expo SDK 55: The Performance Revolution

Released in February 2026, Expo SDK 55 is more than a minor update—it's a major breakthrough for mobile app performance. The most significant change is that Hermes V1 is now the default JavaScript engine. This means all Expo apps automatically benefit from substantial performance improvements.

Compared to traditional JavaScript engines, Hermes V1 offers significantly faster execution and reduced memory consumption. This is particularly impactful on low-spec Android devices. Additionally, new features like Expo Observe, EAS build caching, and the upgraded react-native-screens enhance both development experience and app quality.

This article explores how to implement Expo SDK 55's latest features and optimize your Rork app for superior speed and efficiency.

Hermes V1 Engine: Performance Gains and Implementation

Real Benefits of Hermes V1

Hermes V1 delivers major improvements across multiple dimensions:

| Improvement | Effect | |-------------|--------| | Execution Speed | 15-25% faster than previous engines | | Memory Usage | ~30% reduction in initialization memory | | App Startup | 500ms to 1 second faster startup time | | ES6 Support | Full support for async/await, const/let, and class syntax |

Hermes V1 works by precompiling JavaScript to bytecode, minimizing runtime parsing overhead. This approach is especially powerful on resource-constrained Android devices.

Standard Configuration in Expo SDK 55

With Expo SDK 55, Hermes V1 is automatically enabled. Verify your Expo version:

You can also explicitly configure Hermes in app.json:

After enabling Hermes, APK size typically reduces by 2-3MB thanks to bytecode optimization.

EAS Build Caching: 3x Development Speed Increase

Time Savings with Caching

EAS build caching reuses previous build results when dependencies and configurations remain unchanged. The impact is dramatic:

  • Initial build: 10-15 minutes
  • With cache: 3-5 minutes (70% reduction)

For CI/CD pipelines with frequent builds, this translates to tens of hours saved monthly.

Configuration and Usage

EAS caching is available by default in EAS Build v2+. To maximize efficiency:

Use commands to leverage caching:

Cache persists for 14 days, making it effective for rebuilds within that window.

Modal Improvements and react-native-screens 4.23.0

Why Modal Optimization Matters

Rork apps frequently use modals (bottom sheets, dialogs, navigation stacks). With react-native-screens 4.23.0, native screen lifecycle management improves dramatically:

  • Reduced jank during modal transitions
  • Eliminated memory leaks
  • Smooth animations

Implementation Example: Bottom Sheet

Here's a modern bottom sheet implementation using react-native-screens:

With version 4.23.0, modal stack memory management is automated, preventing leaks even with multiple nested modals.

Expo Observe: Production Performance Monitoring

What is Expo Observe?

Expo Observe (in private preview) monitors real-world app performance through a real-time dashboard, collecting:

  • Frame rate (FPS)
  • Memory usage trends
  • CPU and battery consumption
  • Network request latency
  • Custom metrics

Enabling and Configuring Expo Observe

Initialize Expo Observe by registering your project with EAS Account:

Then initialize the SDK in your app code:

Data uploads to the EAS Dashboard, allowing you to track performance trends over any time period.

Android Blur Optimization: RenderNode API Adoption

Background Blur Processing Evolution

For glassmorphism effects in Rork apps, Expo SDK 55 now leverages the RenderNode API on Android 12+, eliminating complex framebuffer operations and enabling GPU optimization.

Performance improvement:

| Environment | Processing Time | Memory | |-------------|-----------------|--------| | Previous Approach | 16ms/frame | 4.5MB | | RenderNode API | 4ms/frame | 1.2MB |

Implementation Example

Using expo-blur with the latest approach:

Backward compatibility is maintained on Android 12 and below with traditional rendering.

Best Practices: Unified Optimization Strategy

Multi-Layer Optimization Architecture

Here's how to leverage all Expo SDK 55 features together:

  1. Build Layer: EAS caching accelerates development cycles
  2. Runtime Layer: Hermes V1 improves execution performance
  3. UI Layer: Modal and blur optimization reduces jank
  4. Monitoring Layer: Expo Observe tracks production performance

Implementation checklist:

FAQ

Q1: Is Hermes V1 essential? Does it affect debugging?

A: Hermes V1 optimizes production performance. Debug mode automatically selects the appropriate engine. You can disable Hermes during debugging if needed. For performance measurement, enabling Hermes is highly recommended.

Q2: When should cache be cleared?

A: Clear cache after: (1) native dependency updates, (2) node_modules deletion, (3) unexplained build errors. Use eas build --clear-cache.

Q3: What's the overhead of Expo Observe?

A: Overhead is minimal—only 1-2% additional CPU. It's safe for production use.

Q4: Is upgrading from older Expo versions to SDK 55 safe?

A: Generally yes, but test thoroughly. react-native-screens 4.23.0 changes may affect custom modal implementations.

Q5: Is Hermes V1 stable with modern JavaScript syntax?

A: Completely stable. Promise-based code works without issues alongside async/await.

Summary

Expo SDK 55 integrates Hermes V1, EAS build caching, react-native-screens 4.23.0, android-blur optimization, and Expo Observe into a comprehensive performance platform. Development speed and production quality can now coexist, making SDK 55 essential for medium to large Rork app projects.

Implementing the multi-layer optimization strategy progressively will substantially improve your users' experience.
